But in an ideal situation for a right lower lobe, the first step is I lift up on the lung, divide the inferior pulmonary ligament, dissect out the station 9-8 lymph nodes, and isolate the inferior pulmonary vein.

Then I push the lung anterior and dissect out the posterior hilum, which includes the station 7, the subcarinal lymph nodes, and the posterior aspect of the bronchus intermedius, and get that station 11 sump node between the right upper lobe bronchus and the bronchus intermedius, thus exposing the hilum from behind.
